title: phlox subulata subsp. setacea
intro: Phlox subulata subsp. setacea, also known as creeping phlox, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant contains compounds with antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune system function. Creeping phlox is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in folk remedies for skin conditions and respiratory issues.
